framework: A path qualifies only when >=3 prominent, proven people in the field visibly share the capability, each with a resolving link — the pattern across standouts is the evidence, not market demand. Ranked by strength of that pattern. Curated editorial, not DOL-derived.

paths.path: Designing and teaching a scaled-delivery methodology
paths.what_it_is: Codifying your own delivery practice into a repeatable, teachable methodology (an agile coaching curriculum, a scaling framework, a certification course) and personally training other practitioners and organizations to adopt it.
paths.why_it_signals: Multiple independently prominent delivery leaders built their standing the same way — turning personal delivery practice into a licensable, teachable methodology used by thousands of other practitioners — which shows the differentiator is a learnable craft (curriculum design plus train-the-trainer delivery), not innate charisma.

paths.what_it_is: Building and personally hosting a recurring, long-running public teaching format — a podcast, interview series, or education site — that explains and demonstrates delivery practice episode after episode for years, not a single viral post.
paths.why_it_signals: Several senior project and program leaders earned industry-recognized authority through the same mechanism — sustained, disciplined public teaching sustained over a decade or more — which shows the credibility comes from consistent repetition, a habit a new grad can start building immediately rather than something that requires seniority first.

paths.what_it_is: Writing a regular newsletter or blog, grounded in your own day-to-day program or project work, that turns lived delivery experience into named frameworks and playbooks written for peers doing the same job.
paths.why_it_signals: Several working project and technical program managers have differentiated themselves early in their careers this way, showing that externalizing and structuring your own delivery thinking into a public, citable body of work is itself a recognizable and learnable signal of standout capability, distinct from simply doing the job well.
